item.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease Self-Management: An Underappreciated Means to Improve Patient Outcomes.
Cumulative risk of perianal Crohn's disease in population-based cohorts: a systematic review and meta-analysis of twenty-year follow-up.
item.description: BMC public health - Systematic review - Crohn's disease (CD) is an immunologically mediated inflammatory condition affecting the gastrointestinal tract, with pathology involving the entire thickness of the bowel wall. Herein, we reported...

item.title: Long-term classification stability and treatment patterns of inflammatory bowel disease unclassified: a longitudinal observational study.
item.description: BMJ open gastroenterology - Observational study - In patients presenting with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), it may not be possible to diagnose ulcerative colitis (UC) or Crohn's disease (CD) and a classification of IBD Unclassified (IBD-U) is ...

item.title: High Prevalence of MRI Features of Mesenteric Panniculitis in Chronic Intestinal Inflammation: A Retrospective 3-T MRI Study.
item.description: Diagnostics (Basel, Switzerland) - Background/Objectives: Mesenteric panniculitis (MP) is a chronic inflammatory and sclerosing disorder of the mesenteric fat. This study evaluated the occurrence and MRI appearance of MP in patients...

item.title: [Clinical activity and validation of the disability disc in inflammatory bowel disease].
item.description: Revista medica del Instituto Mexicano del Seguro Social - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic immune-mediated pathology that causes disability and can be assessed by the simplified IBD-Disk tool, which could be conditioned by clinical activity. ...

item.title: Medical and surgical therapies for patients with inflammatory bowel diseases and prior or coexisting cancer: a Danish nationwide cohort study.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - As a result of an aging population and the rising incidence of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) across all age groups, more patients with IBD are being diagnosed with cancer both before and after ...
